metadata.title:
Labor Leaders Rush


TO Mayors Defense.

pages.raw_text:
The labor organizations are up in
arms against Rev. George r. Wallace
and Rev. o. w. Van Osdel. who an
nounced yesterday that Mayor
Byrne's administration has failed to
deserve praise from anybody save
the vicious and criminal clements"


This criticism is considered by the
labor leaders as an insult to them,
as it was through organized labor
that Mayor Byrne was elected. His
course while in office has constantly
receive the endorsement of the labor
element, to whom the mayor has been
& sincere friend.


e z. Quinn of the tailors' union
and a prominent member Of the
trades council says:


The ministers will find that they
can not insult the labor people with
impunity. The criminal and vicious
element was meant directly for us,
and we don't propose to be insulted.


THE trades council viii take ac-
ton upon the matter"


Dan Cartano, who has charge of
the official organ of the trades coun-
Gil. said:


Il am not surprised at the insinu-
alton The ministers of this city have
always shown notorious lack of
sympathy for the poorer classes. For
my part, I am heartily in sympathy
with an effort to regulate vice, espe-
cially gambling. which l consider the
workingmans deadliest enemy. But
when the ministers attack Mayor
Byrnes on account of his fidelity to
the laboring people they make a mis-
take. Mayor Byrnes. in my opinion,
has gone as far as any former mayor
in regulating the moral conditions of
the city
